results.name: drannith
results.description: A sanctuary in the Egali plain, on Ikoria.

results.name: drasus
results.description: One of Kytheon's Irregulars, depicted second from the right on the card of the same name.

results.name: draw
results.description: Spells and abilities that textually draw you cards. See also [pure draw](pure-draw).

results.name: drawback
results.description: Cards that have some kind of disadvantage.

results.name: draw engine
results.description: Card draw effects that can net you additional cards repeatedly over time. Contrast [Burst Draw](burst-draw), which nets you multiple cards right away.

results.name: draw hate
results.description: Cards that can hate on players drawing cards.

results.name: drawlink
results.description: Effects that cause combat damage dealt to draw that many cards.

results.name: drink
results.description: A beverage. For the action of drinking, see [drinking](drinking).

results.name: dualism
results.description: Art intentionally highlighting a stark contrast or difference between two things.

results.name: dual wielding
results.description: Because holding two weapons is better than one.

results.name: duggan
results.description: A pugnacious detective who features prominently in the Doctor Who serial "City of Death"

results.name: dungeons and dragons
results.description: Artwork depicting Dungeons and Dragons in any capacity.

results.name: dungeons and dragons goblin
results.description: Goblins from the DnD franchise, including Hobgoblins and Bugbears

results.name: duo
results.description: Art compositions that emphasize a pair of figures. There may yet be more figures beyond the duo in the art. Related but not identical: [two figures](/tags/artwork/two-figures).

results.name: duskmourn nightmare
results.description: Duskmourn nightmares manifest as incarnations of survivors' fears and bad dreams. They hunt and capture their victims, who are often visible in art as terrified faces physically trapped within the nightmare.

results.name: dutch angle
results.description: A tilted view that puts the horizon at a diagonal. Sometimes used to create an effect of disorientation or unease; sometimes just used to make a composition work well.
